rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical decarboxylative sp2-sp3 cross-coupling of redox-active esters (NHPI esters of carboxylic acids) with vinyl iodides using nickel/bipyridine catalysis on Ag-nanoparticle functionalized reticulated vitreous carbon (RVC) cathode with sacrificial Mg anode to assemble polyene/terpene precursors.
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction at Ag-NP-functionalized RVC: (i) initial cathodic reduction of Ag(I) (from AgNO3 + halide) deposits Ag nanoparticles on the RVC surface; (ii) cathodic reduction of Ni(II)(bpy) to lower-valent Ni species which engages the NHPI redox-active ester to generate an alkyl radical via fragmentation, and reduces Ni(III)-alkenyl intermediates to Ni(I)-alkenyl; sacrificial Mg anode is oxidized to Mg(2+).

key_intermediates.name: NHPI redox-active ester (RAE) of carboxylic acid
alkyl radical (R•) from decarboxylative fragmentation of RAE
Ni(II)(bpy)-alkenyl (from oxidative addition of vinyl iodide)
Ag(0) nanoparticles on RVC cathode (electrode modifier)

electron_flow: AgNO3 + halide generates AgCl which is cathodically reduced (0–0.3 F/mol) to deposit Ag nanoparticles on the RVC; thereafter (0.3–2.3 F/mol) the cathode reduces Ni(II)(bpy) to Ni(I), which undergoes oxidative addition with the vinyl iodide and captures the alkyl radical released by single-electron reduction/fragmentation of the NHPI ester, then reductive elimination from Ni(III) delivers the sp2–sp3 product while Mg is oxidized at the anode.

probe_reagents.name: AgNO3 omission (control)
probe_reagents.observation: Without AgNO3 yield drops to 20% (vs 52% with 0.3 equiv AgNO3); induction period correlates with AgNO3 loading, indicating Ag(I) reduction precedes coupling.

probe_reagents.name: Current off / stoichiometric Mg reductant
probe_reagents.observation: No product without current using only Mg as reductant unless Ag is added; confirms electrochemistry is essential.

probe_reagents.name: Pure Ag electrode / Ag-plated electrode (no AgNP)
probe_reagents.observation: Did not work well, whereas Ag-nanoparticles drop-cast on RVC reproduced high yield; identifies nanoparticulate Ag (not bulk Ag) as the active electrode modifier.

probe_reagents.name: LiCl omission / halide source variation
probe_reagents.observation: Halide required to form photosensitive AgCl that decomposes to Ag NPs; pre-stirring AgNO3+LiCl in light before electrolysis gave only 17% yield (photodecomposition diminishes NP formation).

probe_reagents.name: Methyl ester vinyl iodide 40 vs free-acid vinyl iodide 5 (RDE)
probe_reagents.observation: Vinyl iodide 40 + NiCl2(bpy) shows passivation on cycling; with free-acid 5, minimal passivation but a second reduction wave at –1.48 V vs Ag/AgCl appears, assigned to Ni(II/III)-alkenyl → Ni(I)-alkenyl over-reduction (deactivation).

probe_reagents.name: Benzyl chloride reduction on AgNP-GC
probe_reagents.observation: 370 mV positive shift in BnCl reduction potential on AgNP-modified electrode, confirming Ag-NP lowers overpotential of electron-transfer events at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: Doubled catalyst loading (NiCl2/bpy 20 mol%) without AgNO3
probe_reagents.observation: Only 13% yield improvement vs 32% improvement with AgNO3, showing Ag effect is not simply more catalyst turnover.

probe_reagents.name: Constant-potential electrolysis at –1.15 V vs Ag/AgCl without AgNO3
probe_reagents.observation: 10% increase in yield, supporting that limiting cathode potential (as Ag does) avoids over-reduction/passivation.

probe_reagents.name: Intermittent +1.5 V oxidative excursions
probe_reagents.observation: Restored catalytic current and gave 11% yield boost, consistent with stripping of passivating Ni deposits.

notes: The authors articulate four distinct effects of the Ag-NP layer on the cathode (Fig. 4A): (I) adsorption of NiCl2(bpy) catalyst onto Ag-NPs slows electrode passivation; (II) Ag-NPs lower the operative cathode overpotential (–1.15 V vs –1.66 V Ag/AgCl, a 510 mV shift) keeping potential above catalyst-decomposition threshold; (III) Ag-NP film slows diffusion/mass transport of RAE to the electrode (Levich diffusion coefficient halved); (IV) lower overpotential prevents a second, deleterious reduction (Ni(II/III)-alkenyl → Ni(I)-alkenyl) that causes catalyst deactivation via dimerization/aggregation of Ni(I) species. The homogeneous Ni cycle follows literature radical-chain decarboxylative cross-coupling precedent (Ni(I)/Ni(III) or Ni(0)/Ni(II)/Ni(III)); the novel mechanistic contribution is the role of in-situ-formed Ag nanoparticles as an electrode functionalization that engenders selectivity analogous to electroanalytical sensors. Surface characterization (SEM, TEM, EDS, anodic stripping voltammetry) confirms Ag-NP formation; control with pre-photodecomposed AgCl gives no NPs and low yield.
